sections.paragraph:
χειραγωγέω (χεῖρ, ἄγω; Diod S 13, 20, 4; Plut., Maximus Tyr., Lucian et al.; UPZ 110, 55 [164 b.c.]; Judg 16:26 A; Tob 11:16 S; Jos., Ant. 5, 315) take/lead by the hand (e.g. a pers. suddenly blinded by an arrow: Appian, Bell. Civ. 2, 60 §248; a blind pers. in general: Artem. 5, 20) Ac 9:8. Pass. 22:11; GPt 10:40.—M-M. TW. Spicq.

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Maximus Tyr Maximus Tyr , II a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

pers. pers. = person(s)

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

GPt GPt = Gospel of Peter—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1083
